body: PT Bukit Makmur Mandiri Utama, a subsidiary of PT Delta Dunia Makmur Tbk, has secured a contract extension for its mining services at the Meandu Mine in Queensland, Australia, until 2026, with a value of AUD 200 million per year, equivalent to IDR 2.07 trillion. The extension will see Buma Australia maintain its current operational scale, with an annual coal production target of approximately 35 million bank cubic meters, contributing to the local economy and community empowerment.
title: PT Bukit Makmur Mandiri Utama extends mining service contract at Meandu Mine in Australia until 2026, valued at AUD 200 million per year.

body: PT Venteny Fortuna International Tbk has secured a IDR 275 billion credit facility from PT Bank Mayapada Internasional Tbk, which will be used for working capital and capital expenditures, with IDR 100 billion allocated to VTNY and IDR 175 billion to its subsidiary VMI. The company's revenue increased to IDR 87.94 billion in June 2024, up from IDR 58.89 billion in the same period in 2023, but net profit dropped to IDR 3.19 billion from IDR 9.31 billion.
title: PT Venteny Fortuna International Acquires IDR 275 Billion Credit Facility from PT Bank Mayapada Internasional

body: Bank UOB Indonesia projects Indonesia's economy to grow 5.2% in 2024 and 5.3% in 2025, driven by increased foreign direct investment (FDI) and export growth, with a target of 5% year-over-year export growth. The bank aims to facilitate FDI inflows, having brought in approximately SGD 20 billion over the last 5 years, and the government is working to improve the investment climate with relaxations, incentives, and downstreaming projects.
title: UOB Indonesia Projects 5.2% Economic Growth in 2024, Eyes Increase in Foreign Direct Investment and Export Volume

body: Bank UOB Indonesia projects Indonesia's GDP growth to reach 5.2% in 2024 and 5.3% in 2025, driven by positive trends in export growth, especially in natural resources, agriculture, and manufacturing sectors. The bank is committed to supporting foreign investment in primary sectors, including infrastructure, manufacturing, and green technology, to compete with other countries amidst global economic uncertainty.
title: Bank UOB Indonesia Hosts Economic Outlook 2025, Predicts 5.2% GDP Growth in 2024 and 5.3% in 2025

body: PT Hoffmen Cleanindo Tbk, a professional hygiene service provider, reported revenue of IDR 140.65 billion in H1 2024, with a significant increase in net profit to IDR 7.65 billion, driven by an efficiency strategy that boosted its net profit margin to 5.43%. The company's total assets stood at IDR 152.97 billion, with cash and cash equivalents of IDR 13.4 billion, and total equity of IDR 101.13 billion as of June 30, 2024.
title: PT Hoffmen Cleanindo TBK sees significant revenue and profit growth in first half of 2024.

body: PT Pelayaran Kurnia Lautan Semesta Tbk, a logistics delivery company, plans to distribute 2.44 billion shares worth IDR 52.38 billion as stock dividends and 3.68 billion bonus shares worth IDR 60.62 billion, with proposed ratios of 8:1 and 10:7, respectively. The company will seek shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting on October 31, 2024, and the distributions will be taken from its capitalization of earnings.
title: PT Kurnia Lautan Semesta to Distribute 2.44 Billion Stock Dividend Shares and 3.68 Billion Bonus Shares to Shareholders.

body: Following PT Lippo General Insurance's 1:10 stock split, major investors reported significant changes in their share portions, with PT Hanwha Life Insurance Indonesia, PT Inti Anugerah Pratama, and Hanwha General Insurance Co. Ltd. increasing their holdings, while PT Goldman Investindo Sedaya and others reduced their stakes. The changes resulted in shifts in ownership percentages, with some investors increasing their control and others decreasing their shares in various companies, including those in the food, finance, and manufacturing sectors.
title: Lippo General Insurance Conducts 1:10 Stock Split, Leading to Significant Changes in Share Holdings among Major Investors.

body: PT Pertwi Nusantara Raya (PNR), a subsidiary of PT United Tractors, has completed the acquisition of PT Lestari Bumi Papua from two affiliated shareholders for IDR 67.5 billion, transferring approximately 320,145 shares (49.9992%) and 256,125 shares (40.0008%). The transaction aims to diversify PNR's sustainable business activities and advance its development in the forest management and environmental sustainability field.
title: PT Pertwi Nusantara Raya completes acquisition of PT Lestari Bumi Papua for IDR 67.5 billion.

body: The Indonesian government's auction of 7 series of sharia securities or state sukuk received a total of IDR 10 trillion in awarded bids, with the highest bids recorded for SPNS series at IDR 3.38 trillion and PBS series at IDR 9.87 trillion. Since the beginning of 2024, the government has conducted 19 state sukuk auctions, with total incoming bids reaching IDR 398.77 trillion and awarded amounts peaking at IDR 169.87 trillion.
title: Indonesian Government's Sharia Securities Auction Sees IDR 10 Trillion in Total Bids

body: PT Diagnos Laboratorium Utama Tbk's rights issue will increase its paid-in capital to IDR 53.79 billion and portfolio shares to IDR 46.20 billion, with new investors holding a combined 23.82% stake. The company's takeover plan for ASA Ren's shares is valued at IDR 357.89 billion, with an inbreng value of IDR 322.10 billion and a purchase share value of IDR 35.78 billion.
title: PT Diagnos Laboratorium Utama to Conduct Rights Issue, Welcoming New Investors and Changing Shareholder Structure

body: JKon received dividend income from its subsidiary, PT Jaya Beton Indonesia, which has no material impact on the company's operational activities, law, financial condition, or business continuity. In Q1 2024, JKon posted revenue of IDR 749.85 billion and net profit of IDR 23 billion, according to idnfinancials.com data.
title: JKon Receives Dividend Income from Subsidiary PT Jaya Beton Indonesia with No Material Impact on Operations.

body: PT MNC Digital Entertainment Tbk has secured approval for a 1:5 stock split, effective October 7, 2024, aiming to increase transaction flexibility and accessibility for a wider scope of investors. The company's stock price has surged 136.67% since the beginning of the year, closing at IDR 7,100 per share, with the stock split not affecting total share value or shareholders' percentage of total capitalization.
title: MNC Digital Entertainment to Implement 1:5 Stock Split to Boost Liquidity and Accessibility for Wider Investor Base

body: PT Tandan Sawit Papua, a subsidiary of PT Eagle High Plantations Tbk, has secured a IDR 382 billion loan facility from affiliate PT Jaya Mandiri Sukses to reduce its finance expenses and boost financial performance. The loan facility can be converted into equity in the future, according to the company's official statement.
title: PT Tandan Sawit Papua secures IDR 382 billion loan facility from affiliate to reduce finance expenses.

body: The Indonesian government is considering increasing its stake in PT Freeport Indonesia to 10%, with President Joko Widodo stating that the negotiation process will take time, citing the previous 51% share acquisition that took years to complete. The government is pushing for a swift conclusion, with the President urging related ministries to expedite the process, following the inauguration of two smelters worth IDR 77 trillion (USD 5.5 billion) in investment value.
title: Indonesian Government Considers Increasing Stake in PT Freeport Indonesia to 10 Percent

body: Foreign investors sold a net 3.45 billion shares on Friday, September 20, with significant selling volumes in companies like PT GoTo Gojek Tokopedia Tbk, PT Smartfren Telecom Tbk, and PT Bukalapak.com Tbk. Meanwhile, foreign investors purchased notable volumes of shares in companies like PT Bumi Resources Minerals Tbk, PT Maharaksa Biru Energi Tbk, and PT Bank Syariah Indonesia Tbk, among others.
title: Foreign investors in Indonesia sell 14.27 billion shares and buy 10.81 billion shares on Friday, resulting in a net foreign volume of -3.45 billion shares.
